query_strategy: Multiple YouTube search.list requests, each targeting a specific media intent.

query_candidates.media_goal: Find explanatory video, documentary context, archival footage, or scene history.
query_candidates.q: breakbeat DJing Bronx block parties 1974 1976 documentary

request_params.q: breakbeat DJing Bronx block parties 1974 1976 documentary

query_candidates.reason: The event is about the spread of a practice across parties and parks, so explanatory documentary material fits better than a single track.
review_risks: Check that results address Bronx party practice or breakbeat DJing rather than later breakbeat genres.
Prefer results that connect DJing, b-boying, and block-party culture.

query_candidates.media_goal: Find a DJ set, break mix, party mix, turntablism demonstration, or historically framed mix.
query_candidates.q: early hip hop breakbeat DJ mix Bronx block party 1974 1976

request_params.q: early hip hop breakbeat DJ mix Bronx block party 1974 1976

query_candidates.reason: The event describes breakbeat DJ practice spreading across parties, making a breakbeat DJ mix a strong media-intent match.
review_risks: Check whether the mix is historically framed around early hip-hop rather than later breakbeat styles.
Use it as sound context, not as documentation of a specific party.

query_candidates.media_goal: Find a YouTube playlist, mix, or curated song collection that matches the event context.
query_candidates.q: early hip hop breakbeat block party playlist Bronx 1974 1976

request_params.q: early hip hop breakbeat block party playlist Bronx 1974 1976

query_candidates.reason: A breakbeat playlist can support the event's sound/culture axis by representing the records and rhythms used in party practice.
review_risks: Check for modern breakbeat or electronic playlists that do not match the 1974-1976 Bronx context.
Use playlist links as listening context, not as proof of specific party playlists.

omitted_intents.reason: No specific song, artist, or release is named in the event.
The event describes a distributed scene practice rather than one named interview subject.

review_notes: Check whether results are historically relevant before adding links.
Keep all automatically found links as draft until editorial review.
Verify channel/source quality before promoting any media link to reviewed.
